D5 Design L5 Evaluate Reference

Material 3 Tonal Palette

one source hue becomes thirteen tones from 0 to 100 — and the roles (primary, container, on-surface) read straight off them

Material You generates a whole UI palette from a single seed colour. Its HCT space fixes hue and chroma and steps Tone — essentially CIELAB lightness — from 0 (black) to 100 (white). Roles are just named tones: Primary 40, On-Primary 100, Primary-Container 90, On-Container 10.

Set the seed hue and chroma. The ramp shows all thirteen tones; chroma is clamped to gamut at each tone (the lightest and darkest desaturate). This is a close approximation of Material's HCT tonal logic.
